headlineSummary: Anthropic drew clearly negative reaction around trust, support, and PR-driven strategy, while OpenAI was more mixed with some praise for useful tools alongside a mild durability concern [E3][E4][E5][E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was criticized in several comments for poor support, trust issues, and PR-heavy or sloppy behavior, though a couple of remarks still praised Claude’s usefulness and accessibility [E2][E3][E4][E5][E6].
OpenAI had a mixed-neutral tone: commenters praised Codex/GPT for helping users and automating work, but one comment also raised a mild concern that its products may not be long-lived [E1][E2][E6].

evidence.excerpt: I'm increasingly convinced that there's a killer app waiting for whoever can come up with a UI that makes claude code or codex accessible to the average user. Onboarding my non-software engineer teammates to it has super-charged them and essentially given them all their own personal developer that can automate tasks for them.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and praises its impact on non-engineer teammates, while noting a need for better UI/accessibility.
The comment explicitly names Codex and describes these tools as empowering average users and acting like a personal developer, which is positive despite the accessibility caveat.

evidence.excerpt: In fact, I was previously using a different accounting app which had poor API support, so I dumped it so I could use Claude and it's incredible how much this helps me. There is an enormous number of use-cases that Claude/GPT are good for and the hard part is market penetration here.

annotations.rationale: The speaker says using Claude is 'incredible' and emphasizes many good use-cases, indicating strong praise.
The explicit 'Claude/GPT are good for' phrasing positively evaluates GPT use-cases, though the strongest personal praise is directed at Claude.

evidence.excerpt: These are some of the last things I would trust an LLM with in a small business and on top of it Anthropic has shitty customer support. I will actively be telling prospects to avoid.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes Anthropic's customer support and says the speaker will tell prospects to avoid it.

evidence.excerpt: Let me get this straight: a few times per month, someone posts horror stories about how Claude led to losing data and money. Anthropic's response: let's make a nice package out of this, and let's target specifically the businesses that are less likely to be ready to manage such horrible events.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly links Claude to 'losing data and money' and condemns Anthropic for marketing to vulnerable small businesses anyway.

evidence.excerpt: This is only for PR. No one checks what's in those docs, or if these are real, valid or ethical. The goal here is for all news outlets to pick them up. You're not the audience. Given the amount of free PR they can get from some AI-generated .md files, I'd probably do the same if I was on their boat. Right now, I don't think any other AI company generates as much as slop as Anthropic does.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly portrays Anthropic's release as PR-driven and calls its output 'slop,' a strongly negative judgment.

evidence.excerpt: I'm just wondering how committed they'll be - I guess the edge some startups still have, is the fear that product suites from OpenAI / Anthropic / etc. will go the way of Google products, a year or two then straight to the morgue.

annotations.rationale: The comment expresses concern that OpenAI product suites may be short-lived and abandoned quickly, which is a mild negative assessment.
The same concern is applied explicitly to Anthropic, suggesting its vertical products may not be durable or committed long-term.
